experiment: Experiment 5 — Lever-2 on a natural, partially-recoverable substrate
substrate: naturalistic ledger (fallback per prereg §2); the wire convention lives IN the code as an existing to_legacy/from_legacy boundary (keys acct/cents/ts, epoch-int ts, omit-null note, lenient consumer) + a comment that external clients depend on the exact keys. The task neutrally asks for a v2 to_wire/from_wire 'consistent with the codebase'.
model: claude/sonnet x3, 3-hop chain (from_wire needs only to_wire)

manipulation_check.interpretation: Per prereg §4, pointers-only = 8/8 is the H0 outcome: the constraint is re-derived for free. read_cue=8/8 supplies the mechanism — every trial read the in-code to_legacy cue and matched its convention, so success is genuine re-derivation, not luck. A capable same-model receiver does NOT need the residue when the constraint is recoverable from the artifacts.

two_arm_confirm_N12.note: The pre-registered PRIMARY test (+derived vs pointers-only, two arms, PREREG-lever2-natural.md), run at N=12 each in two physically separate worktrees, closing the gap that the 2026-06-20 manipulation-check-only pass left open.

with_derived.injection_verified: architect emitted 5 state.derived.constraint_found items; '## Carried reasoning' confirmed in the downstream to_wire prompt — residue was carried and still added nothing above ceiling

tost_wd_vs_po.method: Newcombe hybrid Wilson-score CI on the risk difference, 90% CI, margin 0.20

two_arm_confirm_N12.data_quality: with-derived: 5 trials ran real, then 7 hit a session rate-limit (turn-1/$0/4s no-ops, architect spawn 'failed'); the 7 were DISCARDED and rerun (2026-07-04) per the program's no-op rule. All 12 reported wd trials are valid real runs.

with_derived_arm: RUN at N=12 (2026-07-04). Result: 12/12, TOST-equivalent to pointers-only 12/12 at ±0.20 (Newcombe diff-CI [-0.184, 0.184]). The residue is carried (injection verified) and unnecessary, the H0 outcome, now via the locked two-arm test rather than the earlier one-sample proxy.
verdict: H0 TOST-CONFIRMED on this substrate: when the constraint is present-and-recoverable in the code (even non-salient), sonnet re-derives it 12/12 pointers-only, and carrying the residue (+derived, injection verified) is statistically equivalent (12/12, TOST at ±0.20), i.e. unnecessary. Paired with Fork-A/Exp-4 (constraint NOT in code, 0% recoverable -> residue necessary, 8/8 vs 0/8, Fisher p<0.001), this BOUNDS Lever 2 from both sides: the derived residue is necessary IFF the reasoning left no recoverable trace in the artifacts. The original design rule ('spend tokens only on what the receiver cannot cheaply regenerate'), now empirically pinned with a confirmed superiority test on one side and a confirmed equivalence test on the other.
limitations: Fallback (semi-synthetic) substrate, chosen for grader cleanliness (exact-key check). The in-code cue here is fairly SALIENT — a named sibling method to_legacy + an explicit 'external clients depend on these keys' comment. A subtler cue (no comment; convention only inferable from usage) might land pointers-only in the partial regime (1-7/8); that boundary is untested.
One substrate, one constraint. The dichotomy (necessary iff non-recoverable) is sharp but rests on Fork-A + this single natural point.
